content:
# Design Document

## Overview

Internal test endpoint `/internal/test-data` replaces direct PostgreSQL access in API tests. Backend owns all data creation. Feature flag `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE` gates access - when disabled, returns 404. When enabled, creates test user and returns data.

**Linus principle**: One data creation path. Tests use HTTP like production. No special cases.

## Steering Document Alignment

### Technical Standards (tech.md)

**Module Pattern Compliance**:
- New `InternalModule` follows standard structure: `controller → service (optional) → database util`
- Reuses existing `UserDBUtil` from `UserModule` - no duplicate logic
- DTOs implement interfaces from `@project/types`
- TypeScript strict mode, no `any` types

**Separation of Concerns**:
- Backend owns data layer (UserDBUtil)
- API tests own HTTP verification only
- Feature flag check isolated in guard (reusable pattern)

**NestJS Best Practices**:
- Guard-based feature flagging (standard NestJS pattern)
- Controller handles HTTP, util handles database
- Module exports nothing (internal-only, no dependencies)

### Project Structure (structure.md)

**New Module Location**:
```
backend/src/modules/internal/
├── controllers/
│   └── internal.controller.ts
├── guards/
│   └── api-test-mode.guard.ts
├── dtos/
│   ├── test-data-response.dto.ts
│   └── index.ts
└── internal.module.ts
```

**Follows Existing Patterns**:
- Guard in `/guards` (like `JwtAuthGuard` in auth module)
- Controller in `/controllers` (like all other controllers)
- DTOs in `/dtos` with barrel export
- Module imports `UserModule` for `UserDBUtil` access

## Code Reuse Analysis

### Existing Components to Leverage

**UserDBUtil** (`backend/src/modules/user/utils/user-db.util.ts`):
- `create()` method handles user creation with TypeORM
- Already handles duplicate ID/email via database constraints
- Returns `UserEntity` with all required fields
- **Usage**: Direct injection in InternalController, call `create()` with test user data

**TEST_USER Constant** (`api-test/src/utils/test-auth.util.ts`):
- Defines test user structure: `{ id, email, name, googleId }`
- **Action**: Move to `@project/types` for backend access
- **Benefit**: Single source of truth for test data

**DTO Pattern** (`backend/src/modules/app/dtos/health-check.dto.ts`):
- Implements interface from `@project/types`
- Constructor accepts params object
- Simple, no validation decorators needed for internal endpoint
- **Usage**: Create `TestDataResponseDTO` following same pattern

### Integration Points

**UserModule** (`backend/src/modules/user/user.module.ts`):
- Already exports `UserDBUtil`
- InternalModule imports UserModule to access util
- Zero new database logic - complete reuse

**TypeORM Database**:
- UserDBUtil uses existing TypeORM connection
- Handles conflicts automatically (duplicate email/id)
- Returns created or existing entity

**Environment Variables**:
- New: `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE` (string: "true" or "false", defaults to disabled)
- Guard reads from `process.env.ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE`
- No ConfigService overhead for single boolean flag

## Architecture

**Request Flow**:
```
HTTP POST /internal/test-data
  → ApiTestModeGuard checks ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE
    → If disabled: throw NotFoundException (404)
    → If enabled: continue
  → InternalController.createTestData()
    → Read TEST_USER constant from @project/types
    → Call UserDBUtil.create() with test data
      → Database insert or return existing (idempotent)
    → Return TestDataResponseDTO with user data
```

**Linus analysis**:
- **Data structure**: Backend owns users, tests consume via HTTP. Simple.
- **Edge cases eliminated**: Idempotent creation via database unique constraints. No special error handling needed.
- **Complexity**: Single guard, single controller method, reuse existing util. 3 new files total.

### Modular Design Principles

**Single File Responsibility**:
- `api-test-mode.guard.ts`: Check environment flag only
- `internal.controller.ts`: HTTP layer only (delegate to UserDBUtil)
- `test-data-response.dto.ts`: Response contract only
- `internal.module.ts`: Module wiring only

**Component Isolation**:
- Guard has zero dependencies (reads env directly)
- Controller depends on UserDBUtil (injected)
- DTOs have zero dependencies
- Module doesn't export anything (internal only)

**Service Layer Separation**:
- **No service layer needed** - controller directly uses UserDBUtil
- Why: Single operation (user creation), no business logic
- Linus principle: Don't add layers for theoretical purity

**No Over-Engineering**:
- No ConfigService for single flag
- No service for single database call
- No validation decorators for internal endpoint
- No authentication (gated by environment flag only)

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[POST /internal/test-data] --> B[ApiTestModeGuard]
    B -->|ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=false| C[404 Not Found]
    B -->|ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=true| D[InternalController]
    D --> E[UserDBUtil.create]
    E --> F[PostgreSQL via TypeORM]
    F --> G[TestDataResponseDTO]
    G --> H[200 OK with user data]
```

## Components and Interfaces

### Component 1: ApiTestModeGuard

**Purpose**: Block requests when feature flag disabled

**Interface**:
```typescript
@Injectable()
export class ApiTestModeGuard implements CanActivate {
  canActivate(): boolean;
}
```

**Implementation**:
```typescript
canActivate(): boolean {
  const enabled = process.env.ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE === 'true';
  if (!enabled) {
    throw new NotFoundException(); // 404, not 403
  }
  return true;
}
```

**Dependencies**: None (reads process.env directly)

**Reuses**: Standard NestJS guard pattern

**Linus verdict**: 5 lines. No special cases. Perfect.

### Component 2: InternalController

**Purpose**: HTTP endpoint for test data creation

**Interface**:
```typescript
@Controller('internal')
export class InternalController {
  @Post('test-data')
  @UseGuards(ApiTestModeGuard)
  async createTestData(): Promise<TestDataResponseDTO>;
}
```

**Implementation**:
```typescript
async createTestData(): Promise<TestDataResponseDTO> {
  const user = await this.userDBUtil.create({
    creationData: TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA, // from @project/types
  });

  return new TestDataResponseDTO({
    id: user.id,
    email: user.email,
    name: user.name,
    googleId: user.googleId,
  });
}
```

**Dependencies**:
- `UserDBUtil` (injected from UserModule)
- `TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA` constant (from @project/types)

**Reuses**:
- Existing UserDBUtil.create() method
- Standard controller pattern

**Error Handling**: None needed. Database constraints handle duplicates, UserDBUtil throws on failure.

### Component 3: TestDataResponseDTO

**Purpose**: Response contract for test data endpoint

**Interface**:
```typescript
export class TestDataResponseDTO {
  id: string;
  email: string;
  name: string;
  googleId: string;

  constructor(params: { id: string; email: string; name: string; googleId: string });
}
```

**Dependencies**: None (or implements interface from @project/types if added)

**Reuses**: Standard DTO constructor pattern

### Component 4: InternalModule

**Purpose**: Module registration and wiring

**Interface**:
```typescript
@Module({
  imports: [UserModule],
  controllers: [InternalController],
})
export class InternalModule {}
```

**Dependencies**: UserModule (for UserDBUtil access)

**Reuses**: Standard NestJS module pattern

**Exports**: Nothing (internal module, no external dependencies)

## Data Models

### TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA (Move to @project/types)

**Current location**: `api-test/src/utils/test-auth.util.ts`

**New location**: `packages/types/src/test-data/test-user.constant.ts`

```typescript
export const TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA = Object.freeze({
  id: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
  email: 'test@mavericks-consulting.com',
  name: 'Test User',
  googleId: 'test-google-id-12345',
} as const);

export type TestUserCreationData = typeof TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA;
```

**Why move**: Backend needs access to create consistent test user

**Breaking change**: Update api-test imports from local constant to @project/types

### TestDataResponseDTO Structure

```typescript
{
  id: string;          // UUID of created/existing test user
  email: string;       // Email from TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA
  name: string;        // Name from TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA
  googleId: string;    // Google ID from TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA
}
```

**No timestamp**: Test data is static, no need for creation time

**No success flag**: HTTP 200 = success, 404/500 = failure

## Error Handling

### Error Scenarios

1. **Feature flag disabled**
   - **Handling**: ApiTestModeGuard throws NotFoundException
   - **User Impact**: 404 Not Found (as if endpoint doesn't exist)
   - **Why 404, not 403**: Don't leak endpoint existence in production

2. **Database connection failure**
   - **Handling**: UserDBUtil throws, NestJS global filter returns 500
   - **User Impact**: 500 Internal Server Error with generic message
   - **No special handling**: Let existing error filters handle it

3. **User already exists (duplicate ID/email)**
   - **Handling**: Database unique constraint, caught by TypeORM, UserDBUtil returns existing
   - **User Impact**: 200 OK with existing user data (idempotent)
   - **Linus principle**: No `if (exists)` check. Let database handle it.

### Non-Errors (No Special Handling Needed)

**Duplicate user creation**: Database returns existing user via ON CONFLICT logic (if implemented) or throws unique constraint error (caught by TypeORM). Either way: idempotent operation.

**Missing environment variable**: Defaults to disabled (safe default). Guard blocks access.

**Invalid JWT in test**: Not our problem. This endpoint has no authentication. Tests manage their own JWT creation.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Testing

**ApiTestModeGuard**:
- Test: `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=true` → canActivate returns true
- Test: `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=false` → throws NotFoundException
- Test: `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=undefined` → throws NotFoundException

**InternalController**:
- Mock UserDBUtil
- Test: createTestData() calls UserDBUtil.create() with TEST_USER_CREATION_DATA
- Test: Returns TestDataResponseDTO with user fields
- No need to test database errors (covered by UserDBUtil tests)

**TestDataResponseDTO**:
- Test: Constructor sets all fields correctly
- Simple constructor test, 1 test case

### Integration Testing

**API Test Migration** (`api-test/src/setup/test-setup.ts`):
- **Before**: Direct PostgreSQL connection, raw SQL insert
- **After**: HTTP POST to `/internal/test-data`, store response

**Test Flow**:
```typescript
// Old (delete this)
const pool = new Pool({ /* db config */ });
await pool.query('INSERT INTO users ...');

// New (replace with this)
const response = await axios.post(`${API_URL}/internal/test-data`);
const testUser = response.data; // Use this in tests
```

**Test cases**:
- Test: POST `/internal/test-data` with `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=true` → 200 OK with user data
- Test: POST `/internal/test-data` with `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=false` → 404 Not Found
- Test: Second call to `/internal/test-data` → Same user data (idempotent)

### End-to-End Testing

**Not applicable**: Internal endpoint only used by test infrastructure

**No E2E needed**: Feature flag prevents production exposure

## Implementation Checklist

**Phase 1: Shared Types**
- [ ] Move TEST_USER constant to `@project/types`
- [ ] Create `TestDataResponseDTO` interface in `@project/types` (optional)
- [ ] Update api-test imports

**Phase 2: Backend Implementation**
- [ ] Create InternalModule directory structure
- [ ] Implement ApiTestModeGuard (5 lines)
- [ ] Implement InternalController (10 lines)
- [ ] Implement TestDataResponseDTO (10 lines)
- [ ] Register InternalModule in AppModule
- [ ] Add `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE` to `.env` file (set to "true" for testing)

**Phase 3: API Test Migration**
- [ ] Update `test-setup.ts` to call HTTP endpoint
- [ ] Remove PostgreSQL connection code
- [ ] Remove `pg` package from api-test dependencies
- [ ] Verify tests pass with new setup

**Phase 4: Cleanup**
- [ ] Delete `api-test/src/setup/test-setup.ts` (if no longer needed)
- [ ] Update documentation
- [ ] Verify `ENABLE_API_TEST_MODE=false` in production .env example

**Total new files**: 5 (guard, controller, DTO, module, shared constant)
**Total deleted files**: 1 (test-setup.ts)
**Net complexity**: Reduced (single HTTP call vs. database connection management)

**Linus verdict**: This eliminates the dual data-creation path. Backend owns users. Tests use HTTP. Dead simple. Ship it.
